content:
{
  "title": "Dynamic Error-Spotting Prompting for Enhanced Mathematical Reasoning in LLMs",
  "problem": "LLMs often make calculation or logic errors in multi-step mathematical problems, and standard prompting methods inadequately mitigate this. Existing prompts do not explicitly encourage LLMs to systematically check their own intermediate reasoning steps.",
  "motivation": "Improving LLM accuracy on mathematics is crucial for domains like STEM education and technical support. Despite large gains from chain-of-thought and self-consistency prompting, error propagation remains a challenge and dynamic error mitigation is underexplored.",
  "method": "We propose a two-phase prompting method: (1) During the solution generation, the LLM is prompted after each intermediate step to explicitly re-express the just-completed step in an alternative format (e.g., symbolic vs. verbal; forward vs. backward calculation). (2) If a mismatch is detected between the two expressions, the LLM is prompted to re-examine and revise the step until consistency is achieved. This dynamic, self-auditing prompting encourages the model to actively check and repair its reasoning at each stage, reducing error accumulation.",
  "experiment_plan": "We evaluate on GSM8K and MATH datasets, comparing against chain-of-thought, self-consistency, and reflection prompts. Metrics include final answer accuracy, stepwise correctness, and error detection/repair rates. Human evaluation will rate solution clarity and error spotting. Ablations will isolate the effects of alternative re-expression and dynamic repair."
}
